channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iranian Americans in Los Angeles Shift from Celebration to Concern Over Potential Chaos in Iran After Khamenei's Death
item.description: Following the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, Iranian American communities in Los Angeles, especially in Westwood's 'Tehrangeles' area, initiall...

item.title: PM Modi Praises Om Birla for Managing Lok Sabha with Fairness and Patience
item.description: In a strong show of support just two days ahead of a resolution seeking his removal, Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla received commendation from Prime Minister Narendra Modi. During a virtual address at the...

item.title: KeNHA Clarifies Green Park Pedestrian Underpass Remains Safe after Rain, AI Photo Misleads Public
item.description: The Kenyan National Highways Authority (KeNHA) has moved swiftly to debunk a viral image claiming that the newly-built Green Park pedestrian underpass in Nairobi flooded after heavy rainfall. The phot...

item.title: US Declares Emergency to Expedite Bomb Shipments to Israel Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: The US State Department has invoked emergency authority to bypass Congress and sell more than 20,000 bombs to Israel, valued at nearly US$660 million, amid escalating conflict with Iran. Secretary of ...

item.title: Government Implements Measures to Support Distressed Municipalities
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile has outlined ongoing government initiatives to stabilise municipalities facing persistent service delivery and governance challenges. Municipalities such as Ditsobotla ...

item.title: Governor Abiodun Urges Muslims to Practice Compassion and Service During Ramadan
item.description: Oga Dapo Abiodun, Ogun State Governor, don urge all Muslims make the spirit of Ramadan shine for their everyday life. Na during one special Ramadan Iftar wey the state government organise, dem use the...

item.title: Brad Gushue exits Brier after playoff loss to Brad Jacobs
item.description: In St. John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador, Brad Gushue’s illustrious curling career at the Brier has come to a close. The six-time Canadian champion was knocked out of the tournament following a 7-5 pl...

item.title: Chinese businessman kidnapped in Bacoor, Cavite while family restrained during robbery
item.description: A Chinese businessman was abducted in Bacoor, Cavite after seven armed men broke into his home on Thursday night. The attackers tied up his wife, three children aged 4, 10, and 13, and three other fam...

item.title: Eze Condemns Attack on ADC Secretariat in Amaechi's Hometown, Blames Tinubu and Wike
item.description: A chieftain of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Eze Chukwuemeka Eze, has come out hard to condemn the violent attack on the African Democratic Congress (ADC) secretariat for Ubima Ward in Rivers S...

item.title: Mourners Escape Injuries as Bus is Swept by Flooded Mogondo River after Burial in Emurua Dikirr
item.description: On Friday, March 6, a frightening incident occurred in Emurua Dikirr during the burial of late MP Johana Ng'eno, who had tragically died in a helicopter crash on February 28. Hundreds of mourners had ...

item.title: Conservative Support Drops in Alberta, Liberals Gain Ground, Poll Shows
item.description: A recent Abacus Data poll indicates a significant shift in voter intentions in Alberta, a province traditionally dominated by the Conservative Party. The poll shows the Conservatives with 51% of the v...

item.title: Heavy rains trigger floods, deaths and road chaos in Nairobi
item.description: Heavy overnight rains have caused severe flash floods in Nairobi, leaving at least 23 people dead and dozens of others rescued as rivers overflowed and streets turned into rivers. Major roads such as ...

item.title: Tanzanian Women Achieve Milestones in Mining and Leadership
item.description: On this year’s International Women’s Day, Tanzania celebrated the remarkable achievements of women making waves in mining and leadership. Leminatha Kabigumila, CEO and founder of RARE Mining East Afri...

item.title: Seven-year-old boy tragically killed in Staffordshire road accident
item.description: A seven-year-old boy has tragically died after being struck by a car on the A38 near Lichfield, Staffordshire. The incident occurred at around 5am on Saturday at the junction with the A5206. Emergency...

item.title: AFP reminds foreign visitors to secure clearance before accessing military camp facilities
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) has reminded foreign nationals that obtaining a visit clearance is mandatory before entering recreational facilities in military camps, such as golf courses. ...

item.title: Government launches free electricity subsidy for 4Ps beneficiaries in Iloilo
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD), in coordination with the Department of Energy (DOE) and the Energy Regulatory Commission (ERC), has launched the Expanded Lifeline Rate Subsidy...

item.title: Delhi CM reviews rollout of free travel Saheli Pink Smart Card for women and transgender residents
item.description: On Saturday, Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta personally travelled on a Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) bus to monitor the implementation of the Saheli Pink Smart Card scheme. Launched earlier this ...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens cooperation with Commonwealth nations on governance and development
item.description: Tanzania has once again confirmed its dedication to fostering strong relations with members of the Commonwealth, focusing on good governance, democracy, and shared development. Dis way was made clear ...

item.title: New Zealand vow to silence Indian crowd as India seek to defend T20 World Cup title in Ahmedabad final
item.description: New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner has said his team are ready to silence the massive Indian crowd when they face India in the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad...

item.title: Orange Sky marks a decade supporting Canberra residents with mobile laundry service
item.description: Orange Sky has been running a mobile laundry service in Canberra for the past ten years, offering not just clean clothes but also human connection for people experiencing homelessness or hardship. Vol...
